codeReview.summary: The diff is minimal and correct: exactly 2 lines changed in uv.lock — the ypricemagic version string (dev1485 → dev1486) and the pinned git hash (d1dfd45636935f7f7884c5730e337d5731d0aa94 → 35eba38cb3343a83c5dd4491793d3902cfdcf65f). pyproject.toml was not touched, confirming the @master ref stays intact. All four expectedBehavior items are satisfied.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill's Step 3 ('Write Tests First (TDD)') has no carve-out for dependency-only tasks. The worker correctly skipped TDD here (no code-path changed), but had to justify the deviation manually. The skill should explicitly state that TDD is skipped when the task is a lockfile-only update with no source code changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false; deviations[0].step='3. Write Tests First (TDD)'; suggested change: 'For dependency-only backend tasks, the skill should explicitly allow skipping the TDD step and focus on lockfile diff inspection plus validator reruns.'

summary: Clean pass. Commit 131b29d contains exactly the expected change: uv.lock updated from ypricemagic dev1485@d1dfd456 to dev1486@35eba38c, with no other file modified. Worker ran all verification steps (pytest 272 passed, ruff clean, mypy clean) before committing. The only deviation from the backend-worker skill (skipping TDD) was fully justified and appropriate for a lockfile-only task.
